devfleet-status.description: Show devfleet runner health, running/pending tasks, and schedules.
devfleet-status.template: Use the devfleet MCP tools to produce a concise devfleet operations status report. Call devfleet_runner_health with include_tasks=true, devfleet_list_tasks for status=running with limit=20, devfleet_list_tasks for status=pending with limit=20, and devfleet_list_schedules with enabled_only=false. Summarize fleet activity, runner image versions, running/stale/pending task counts, each active task, and all schedules. If an MCP call fails, include the exact error and continue. Do not edit files.

devfleet-tasks.description: List recent Devfleet tasks with status summary.
devfleet-tasks.template: Run devfleet_list_tasks to get recent tasks. Summarize: which are running, done, errored, and pending. For running tasks note if any are stale (>10min since last event). Keep output concise.

devfleet-submit.description: Submit a new task to the Devfleet runner fleet.
devfleet-submit.template: Ask the user for the task prompt, then submit via devfleet_submit_task. Ask for git_url if not obvious, default git_ref to main. Suggest agent_image if known. Let the user optionally specify agent, provider_id, model_id, and timeout_sec. Return the task ID.

devfleet-schedules.template: Run devfleet_list_schedules to list all cron task schedules. Compare enabled vs disabled counts and show the name, cron expression, and prompt summary for each schedule. Keep output concise.

devfleet-cancel.description: Cancel a pending or running Devfleet task.
devfleet-cancel.template: Cancel the specified devfleet task. Ask for the task ID if not provided. Use devfleet_cancel_task with the given ID and optionally a reason. Confirm the result.
